sections.title: Turn Order
rules: GM tells players who is in danger

actions.name: Attack
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Attack Stat. If the roll is lower, deal 1 @Physical Damage (2 for a natural 1, aka a crit). If your @Insight is too low to perceive a monster as it is, they take -1 damage from your attacks.

actions.name: Dodge
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Agility. If the roll is lower, immune to dodgeable attacks until next turn and regain 1 @Hit Points.

actions.name: Brace
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Body. If the roll is lower and you have a weapon, armor, or shield, you are immune to braceable attacks until next turn and regain 1 @Hit Points.

actions.name: Draw a Power
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Focus. If the roll is lower, gather 1 @Power from your @Power deck. If you roll a 10 you lose 1 @Hit Points, and discard all held @Power cards.

actions.name: Flee
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Agility. If the roll is lower, you can successfully exit the combat and cannot be in danger for the next round. If the roll fails, you remain in combat.

actions.name: Negotiate
actions.description: Roll 1d10 against @Focus. If the roll is lower, combat will end momentarily to present the opportunity to negotiate with a monster that can understand you. If combat resumes after negotiations fail, the monster(s) go first.

sections.title: Weapons
sections.description: Weapons do not have extensive effects on combat. Two-weapons, polearms, and ranged weapons, or special weapons let you roll with @Advantage when attacking.

sections.title: Advantage and Disadvantage
rules: When you have @Advantage, roll 2d10 and choose the lower result for your roll
When you have @Disadvantage, roll 2d10 and choose the higher result for your roll
@Advantage and @Disadvantage cancel each other out - if you have both, roll normally with 1d10

examples: Using specialized weapons (two-weapons, polearms, ranged weapons)
Attacking from a position of superiority (flanking, high ground)
Using equipment or powers that grant benefits
Fighting in poor conditions (darkness, difficult terrain)

types.name: Physical Damage
types.description: Standard @Damage from conventional weapons such as swords, guns, clubs, and other mundane armaments. Most creatures can be harmed by @Physical Damage, though some may have resistance or immunity to certain types of @Physical Damage attacks.

types.name: Spiritual Damage
types.description: @Damage that affects the soul or essence of a creature. Particularly effective against undead, demons, and other supernatural entities. Some creatures may be vulnerable to @Spiritual Damage while being resistant to @Physical Damage attacks.

types.name: Hybrid Damage
types.description: @Damage that combines both @Physical Damage and @Spiritual Damage elements, making it effective against a wider range of creatures. These weapons and attacks can harm both corporeal and incorporeal beings.

sections.title: Range
sections.description: Distance in I Must Kill is described in three abstract bands rather than feet or meters. These ranges apply to attacks, powers, and targeting.

types.name: Close
types.description: Within arm's reach — close enough to touch, grapple, or strike without stepping. Most melee weapons and unarmed attacks operate at @Close range.

types.name: Near
types.description: Within the same room or immediate area — you could cross the distance in a few strides. Most thrown weapons, short-range powers, and many creature abilities operate at @Near range.

types.name: Far
types.description: Across a field or large open space — visible but requiring significant effort to close the gap. Ranged weapons, bows, firearms, and long-range powers operate at @Far range.

sections.title: Size
sections.description: All creatures in I Must Kill belong to one of five size categories. Size affects how much damage hunters can deal and how easily they can avoid attacks from massive creatures.

types.name: Tiny
types.description: From the size of an insect to a house cat. Hunters deal +1 damage to anything @Tiny compared to themselves — if they remember to ask for it.

types.name: Smaller
types.description: A large dog to a human child in size. Hunters deal +1 damage to anything @Smaller than themselves — if they remember to ask for it.

types.name: Your Size
types.description: Roughly the size of an adult human. This is the baseline for hunters — no size-based damage bonuses or penalties apply when fighting creatures of a similar size.

types.name: Bigger
types.description: As big as a horse. @Bigger creatures are large enough to ride. Hunters gain no size bonus when attacking @Bigger creatures.

types.name: Way Bigger
types.description: Anything larger than a small hut — you can climb them. When a @Way Bigger creature attacks a hunter, that hunter may roll an extra d10 and choose the lower result when @Dodging.

equipment.name: Shield
equipment.effect: Roll an additional d10 when bracing, choose the lower.

equipment.name: Armor
equipment.effect: Roll an additional d10 when bracing, choose the lower.

conditions.name: Frightened
conditions.description: A creature that is @Frightened cannot @Attack on their turn or willingly move towards the source of their fear if there's another route of escape.

conditions.name: Unconscious
conditions.description: A creature cannot defend against an @Attack, or make @Attack actions, or move. @Attack actions against an @Unconscious creature automatically succeed. Creatures that survive an @Attack while @Unconscious may awaken at the start of their next turn.

conditions.name: Diseased
conditions.description: When diseased, characters make all tests with @Disadvantage. This represents illness, poison, or other debilitating conditions that weaken the character's performance.

conditions.name: Obscured
conditions.description: A creature that is @Obscured cannot be targeted by attacks or powers. A creature is @Obscured when it is behind full cover — such as a solid barricade, a wall, or inside a building relative to the attacker. Partial cover does not grant this condition. An @Obscured creature may still be able to act, move, or target others if they have a way to attack from their position.
